goal: Land `LancedbChunkIndexer` — the per-branch background chunk-indexing pipeline that consumes `ucil_embeddings::EmbeddingChunker` + `ucil_embeddings::CodeRankEmbed` (the latter behind a UCIL-internal `EmbeddingSource` trait per DEC-0008 §4 — TestEmbeddingSource impl in tests is NOT a critical-dep mock per WO-0048 lessons line 363) and writes 12-column `RecordBatch` rows into the per-branch `code_chunks` LanceDB table opened by `BranchManager::create_branch_table` (WO-0053 / P2-W7-F09; merged to main via DEC-0016 §Closed at commit `57e50ab`). The indexer skips unchanged files via a per-branch `indexer-state.json` mtime sidecar and emits a NEW `file_hash` column (master-plan §12.2 line 1335 — content-addressed delta key) computed via `sha2::Sha256` over file contents.
plan_summary: Add `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lancedb_indexer.rs` (NEW module). Define `pub trait EmbeddingSource: Send + Sync { async fn embed(&self, code: &str) -> Result<Vec<f32>, EmbeddingSourceError>; }` (UCIL-internal seam per DEC-0008 §4). Implement `pub struct CodeRankEmbeddingSource { embedder: Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<CodeRankEmbed>> }` (production impl wrapping `ucil_embeddings::CodeRankEmbed` — Mutex required because `CodeRankEmbed::embed` takes `&mut self` per WO-0058 lessons line 561 + WO-0059 lessons). Implement `pub struct LancedbChunkIndexer<S: EmbeddingSource>` with fields `branch_manager: Arc<BranchManager>`, `branch_name: String`, `chunker: Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<EmbeddingChunker>>` (Mutex required because `EmbeddingChunker::chunk` takes `&mut self` per WO-0060 lessons line 658), `source: Arc<S>`, and `state: IndexerState`. The public surface is `pub async fn index_paths(&mut self, repo_root: &Path, paths: &[PathBuf]) -> Result<IndexerStats, ChunkIndexerError>` which: (1) loads the persisted `IndexerState` JSON sidecar at `<branches_root>/<sanitised_branch>/indexer-state.json`; (2) for each path, reads `tokio::fs::metadata(&path).await?.modified()?` and compares against `state.file_mtimes.get(&path)` — if equal, increments `files_skipped_unchanged` and continues; (3) otherwise reads the file, computes `Sha256` digest hex (via NEW workspace dep `sha2 = "0.10"`), runs `chunker.lock().await.chunk(content, language, repo_relative_path)`, embeds each chunk via `source.embed(&chunk.content).await`, builds a 12-column Arrow `RecordBatch` matching `branch_manager::code_chunks_schema()` (`id`, `file_path`, `start_line`, `end_line`, `content`, `language`, `symbol_name`, `symbol_kind`, `embedding` as `FixedSizeListArray<Float32, 768>`, `token_count`, `file_hash`, `indexed_at` as `TimestampMicrosecondArray`), opens the LanceDB connection at `branch_manager.branch_vectors_dir(&branch_name)`, calls `conn.open_table("code_chunks").execute().await?.add(record_batch_iter).execute().await?`, and updates `state.file_mtimes.insert(path.clone(), mtime_secs)`; (4) atomically persists state to `indexer-state.json.tmp` then `tokio::fs::rename` to `indexer-state.json` (crash-safe write per WO-0021 lifecycle precedent). Add a `pub struct IndexerHandle` that wraps `LancedbChunkIndexer` in a `tokio::task::spawn`-able loop subscribed to a `tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<watcher::FileEvent>` and dispatches each `FileEvent::Created/Modified` to `indexer.index_paths(...)` — proves the 'background' half of F04's description without coupling the frozen unit test to the watcher. Add the frozen acceptance test `pub async fn test_lancedb_incremental_indexing()` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` (per DEC-0007 — selector `executor::test_lancedb_incremental_indexing` resolves to `ucil_daemon::executor::test_lancedb_incremental_indexing` ONLY when the fn is column-0 in `executor.rs`, NOT inside `mod tests {}`). The test exercises 6 sub-assertions: SA1 first-pass-indexes-all, SA2 second-pass-skips-unchanged, SA3 touch-then-reindex-only-touched, SA4 file_hash-differs-on-content-change, SA5 persisted-state-json-roundtrips, SA6 embedding-column-is-FixedSizeList<Float32,768>. Add a second module-root test `executor::test_lancedb_indexer_handle_processes_events` exercising the watcher→indexer wiring via a real `tokio::sync::mpsc` channel. Apply the W8-cohort discipline carried in `scope_in[14..18]`: pre-flight ≤70-char commit-subject validation per WO-0059 lessons line 607, ban-words pre-flight on `mock|fake|stub|fixture` per WO-0061 lessons line 696, upstream-API research checklist per WO-0060 lessons line 644 + 567 + 658, RFR `Verifier-universal gates` section per WO-0059 lessons line 615, and pinned `coverage-gate.sh ucil-daemon 85 75` per WO-0060 lessons line 643.
scope_in: Add `ucil-embeddings = { path = "../ucil-embeddings" }` to `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` `[dependencies]`. This is the FIRST consumer of the `ucil-embeddings` crate from `ucil-daemon`. Add a brief block-comment above the dep line: '`ucil-embeddings` provides `CodeRankEmbed` (default 137M Int8-quantised embedding model, P2-W8-F02, WO-0059) and `EmbeddingChunker` (real-tokenizer chunker, P2-W8-F05, WO-0060) consumed by `lancedb_indexer.rs` (P2-W8-F04, this WO). The dep transitively pulls `ort 2.x` with the `download-binaries` CPU EP per WO-0058 — first-build cargo wall-time inflation by 30-90s is expected per WO-0058 lessons line 565.'

Add `sha2 = "0.10"` to `[workspace.dependencies]` in the workspace `Cargo.toml`. This is a NEW workspace-level dep used to compute the `file_hash` column value per the master-plan §12.2 line 1335 schema (`file_hash` drives delta re-indexing in P2-W8-F04). Brief block-comment: '`sha2` provides `Sha256` for the `file_hash` column of `code_chunks` (master-plan §12.2 line 1335). Used ONLY by `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lancedb_indexer.rs` (P2-W8-F04, WO-0064). Pin `0.10` (current stable line as of 2026-05-07).' Then add `sha2.workspace = true` to `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` `[dependencies]`. Cargo.lock churn is expected per WO-0058 lessons line 569 / WO-0061 lessons line 702 — accepted-as-documented.

Create `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lancedb_indexer.rs` (NEW file). Module-level `//!` doc cites: master-plan §12.2 lines 1321-1346 (`code_chunks` table schema), §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1789 (background chunk indexing description), DEC-0008 (UCIL-internal trait boundaries — `EmbeddingSource` is the seam), DEC-0016 §Closed (merge `57e50ab` made `BranchManager` reachable on main — F04 directly consumes `BranchManager::create_branch_table` + `branch_vectors_dir` + `code_chunks_schema`), WO-0053 (per-branch table lifecycle), WO-0058 (`OnnxSession` foundation), WO-0059 (`CodeRankEmbed` model), WO-0060 (`EmbeddingChunker`). Define `pub enum ChunkIndexerError` via `thiserror::Error` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`, with variants:  
  - `Io { #[from] source: std::io::Error }` — fs reads/writes/metadata.  
  - `Json { #[from] source: serde_json::Error }` — state JSON parse / serialize.  
  - `BranchManager { #[from] source: BranchManagerError }` — branch table open/list failures.  
  - `Lance { #[from] source: lancedb::Error }` — LanceDB connection / open_table / add failures.  
  - `Arrow { #[from] source: arrow_schema::ArrowError }` — `RecordBatch::try_new` errors.  
  - `Embedding { #[from] source: EmbeddingSourceError }` — embedding source failures bubble up via the trait.  
  - `Chunker { #[from] source: ucil_embeddings::EmbeddingChunkerError }` — chunker errors bubble up.  
  - `DimensionMismatch { expected: usize, got: usize, file: PathBuf }` — embedding dim != `EMBEDDING_DIM` (defensive — proves the trait contract is honoured).  
  - `MtimeUnsupported { file: PathBuf }` — `fs::Metadata::modified()` returned `Err` on a platform without modification-time support (defensive — Unix/Windows always support it; this is a load-bearing OPS-friendly diagnostic per WO-0044 standing pattern).  
  Each variant ships a substantive `#[error(...)]` message naming the offending file path / dimension when relevant. Mark `#[non_exhaustive]` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`. Define `pub enum EmbeddingSourceError` (also `thiserror::Error`, `#[non_exhaustive]`) with variants `CodeRankEmbed { #[from] source: ucil_embeddings::CodeRankEmbedError }` and `Other { message: String }` (escape hatch for downstream impls). Pre-bake one negative-path unit test PER reachable variant per WO-0059 lessons line 606 / WO-0060 lessons line 635 — list each variant in a `## Variant coverage map` block in the RFR.

Inside `lancedb_indexer.rs`, define `#[async_trait::async_trait] pub trait EmbeddingSource: Send + Sync { fn name(&self) -> &str; fn dim(&self) -> usize; async fn embed(&self, code: &str) -> Result<Vec<f32>, EmbeddingSourceError>; }`. The `dim()` accessor lets the indexer validate `embedding.len() == dim()` BEFORE constructing the FixedSizeList — defensive bounds-check that surfaces a clear `ChunkIndexerError::DimensionMismatch` instead of letting Arrow panic on length-mismatch (`FixedSizeList<Float32, 768>` is fixed-width, so a 0-len or wrong-len Vec would trigger an opaque arrow error). Document the trait's contract in rustdoc — production callers must wrap `&mut`-shared embedders in `tokio::sync::Mutex` per WO-0058 lessons line 561 (the `&self` shape on the trait keeps the call-site clean).

Inside `lancedb_indexer.rs`, define `pub struct CodeRankEmbeddingSource { embedder: Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<ucil_embeddings::CodeRankEmbed>>, model_dir: PathBuf }`. Implement `pub fn load(model_dir: &Path) -> Result<Self, EmbeddingSourceError>` calling `ucil_embeddings::CodeRankEmbed::load(model_dir).map_err(|e| EmbeddingSourceError::CodeRankEmbed { source: e })?` and wrapping in `Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(embedder))`. Implement `EmbeddingSource` for `CodeRankEmbeddingSource` — `name() -> "coderankembed"`, `dim() -> ucil_embeddings::EMBEDDING_DIM` (768), and `async fn embed(&self, code: &str)` acquires the Mutex, calls `embedder.embed(code)` (sync API per WO-0059 — wrap in `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` if blocking-on-runtime concerns surface, OR document inline that the Mutex-locked sync call is acceptable for the F04 sequential-batch shape since the indexer is itself batched per-file). Acceptable per WO-0058 lessons line 561 (`&mut self → Arc<Mutex<>>`). Disclose the Mutex shape in the RFR's upstream-API research checklist per WO-0060 lessons line 644.

Inside `lancedb_indexer.rs`, define `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)] pub struct IndexerState { pub file_mtimes: std::collections::BTreeMap<std::path::PathBuf, u64>, pub schema_version: u32 }`. The `BTreeMap` (NOT `HashMap`) gives deterministic iteration order — JSON serialization is byte-stable across runs which simplifies golden-file diffs. `schema_version: 1` for this WO; bumping triggers re-index of all files (graceful migration). Path keys are repo-root-relative so the JSON is portable. mtime values are seconds-since-Unix-epoch (stable across systems; no nanosecond precision needed for incremental-indexing semantics). Add `pub fn load_or_default(path: &Path) -> Result<Self, ChunkIndexerError>` that reads + parses the JSON if present, returns `Default::default()` if `try_exists` returns `Ok(false)`, returns `Err(ChunkIndexerError::Io { ... })` on read errors that are NOT `NotFound`. Add `pub async fn save_atomic(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<(), ChunkIndexerError>` that writes to `<path>.tmp` then `tokio::fs::rename` to `<path>` (atomic on POSIX same-fs per `tokio::fs::rename` contract). Add `pub fn schema_version_current() -> u32 { 1 }` constant accessor for parity tests.

Inside `lancedb_indexer.rs`, define `#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)] pub struct IndexerStats { pub files_scanned: usize, pub files_skipped_unchanged: usize, pub files_indexed: usize, pub chunks_inserted: usize, pub chunks_failed: usize }`. The `chunks_failed` counter tracks per-chunk embedding failures that DO NOT fail the whole batch (graceful degradation — log + continue per master-plan §15 partial-failure pattern). Per-file embedding errors STILL fail the whole batch (the file's chunks are a coherent unit).

Inside `lancedb_indexer.rs`, define `pub struct LancedbChunkIndexer<S: EmbeddingSource> { branch_manager: Arc<BranchManager>, branch_name: String, chunker: Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<ucil_embeddings::EmbeddingChunker>>, source: Arc<S>, state: IndexerState }`. The generic-over-`S` shape lets tests inject a deterministic `TestEmbeddingSource` while production wires `CodeRankEmbeddingSource`. Public constructors:  
  - `pub fn new(branch_manager: Arc<BranchManager>, branch_name: impl Into<String>, chunker: Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<ucil_embeddings::EmbeddingChunker>>, source: Arc<S>) -> Self` — initial-state `IndexerState::default()`.  
  - `pub async fn load_state(&mut self) -> Result<(), ChunkIndexerError>` — reads `<branches_root>/<sanitised_branch>/indexer-state.json` via `IndexerState::load_or_default`. Helper: `pub fn state_path(&self) -> PathBuf { self.branch_manager.branches_root().join(sanitise_branch_name(&self.branch_name)).join("indexer-state.json") }` — note the duplicate of `branch_manager.rs:350`'s private `sanitise_branch_name`; per branch_manager.rs:347-349 ('intentional duplication so `branch_manager.rs` is self-contained — a shared helper would require a new public symbol surface and is deferred to a follow-up ADR if a third caller materialises'), F04 IS the third caller — so this WO promotes `sanitise_branch_name` to `pub fn` in `branch_manager.rs` (a one-liner change documented inline + re-exported from `lib.rs`). Cite branch_manager.rs:347-349 in the commit body.  
  - `pub fn stats(&self) -> &IndexerState` — read-only state accessor.

Inside `lancedb_indexer.rs`, implement the core pipeline:  
  ```
  #[tracing::instrument(
      name = "ucil.executor.lancedb_index",
      level = "debug",
      skip(self, paths),
      fields(
          branch = %self.branch_name,
          path_count = paths.len(),
          source = %self.source.name(),
      ),
  )]
  pub async fn index_paths(
      &mut self,
      repo_root: &Path,
      paths: &[PathBuf],
  ) -> Result<IndexerStats, ChunkIndexerError>
  ```
  Body:  
  1. `self.load_state().await?` (re-load so concurrent writers don't get stale state — defensive).  
  2. Open the LanceDB connection ONCE per call: `let vectors_dir = self.branch_manager.branch_vectors_dir(&self.branch_name); let uri = vectors_dir.to_str().ok_or_else(|| ChunkIndexerError::Lance { source: lancedb::Error::Other { message: format!("non-UTF8 vectors dir: {:?}", vectors_dir), source: None } })?; let conn = lancedb::connect(uri).execute().await?; let table = conn.open_table("code_chunks").execute().await?;` — open_table fails if `BranchManager::create_branch_table` was not called first; that's the load-bearing precondition documented in the function's rustdoc.  
  3. Initialize `let mut stats = IndexerStats::default();` and a `let mut row_buffer: Vec<RowDraft> = Vec::new();` to batch rows across files.  
  4. For each `path in paths`:  
     - `stats.files_scanned += 1`.  
     - Compute `repo_rel = path.strip_prefix(repo_root).unwrap_or(path).to_owned();` (defensive — fall back to absolute if not under root).  
     - `let metadata = tokio::fs::metadata(path).await?;` then `let mtime_systemtime = metadata.modified().map_err(|_| ChunkIndexerError::MtimeUnsupported { file: repo_rel.clone() })?;` then `let mtime_secs = mtime_systemtime.duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH).map(|d| d.as_secs()).unwrap_or(0);`.  
     - If `self.state.file_mtimes.get(&repo_rel) == Some(&mtime_secs)`: increment `stats.files_skipped_unchanged` and `tracing::debug!("skipping unchanged file {:?}", repo_rel);` and continue.  
     - Otherwise: read the file via `let content_bytes = tokio::fs::read(path).await?; let content = String::from_utf8_lossy(&content_bytes).into_owned();` (lossy because non-UTF8 source files should still index — tree-sitter handles bad UTF8 by treating bytes as opaque).  
     - Compute `let file_hash = format!("{:x}", sha2::Sha256::digest(&content_bytes));` (hex digest, 64 chars).  
     - Infer language from extension: `let language = infer_language(path);` — reuse `crate::executor::infer_language(...)` if it exists at module root; otherwise inline a copy of the existing match-on-extension shape from `executor.rs:78-90` (the existing `IngestPipeline::infer_language` private fn — promote to `pub(crate) fn infer_language(path: &Path) -> Option<&'static str>` if needed, document inline in the commit body if the promotion is required).  
     - `let chunks = self.chunker.lock().await.chunk(&content, language.unwrap_or("unknown"), &repo_rel.to_string_lossy()).map_err(|e| ChunkIndexerError::Chunker { source: e })?;` — the `EmbeddingChunker::chunk` signature was confirmed at WO-0060 to take `&mut self` (per upstream-API research) — Mutex acquire required.  
     - For each `chunk in chunks`:  
       - `let embedding = match self.source.embed(&chunk.content).await { Ok(v) => v, Err(e) => { tracing::warn!("embedding failed for chunk {} of {:?}: {}; skipping chunk", chunk.id, repo_rel, e); stats.chunks_failed += 1; continue; } };`.  
       - If `embedding.len() != self.source.dim()`, return `Err(ChunkIndexerError::DimensionMismatch { expected: self.source.dim(), got: embedding.len(), file: repo_rel.clone() })` (whole-batch fail because dim drift indicates a model misconfig, not a transient chunk error).  
       - Push a `RowDraft { id: chunk.id.clone(), file_path: repo_rel.to_string_lossy().into_owned(), start_line: chunk.start_line, end_line: chunk.end_line, content: chunk.content.clone(), language: chunk.language.clone(), symbol_name: chunk.symbol_name.clone(), symbol_kind: chunk.symbol_kind.clone(), embedding, token_count: chunk.token_count, file_hash: file_hash.clone(), indexed_at_micros: chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_micros() }` onto `row_buffer`. (Note: `chrono` is already a workspace dep per Cargo.toml line 99; `arrow-array::TimestampMicrosecondArray` accepts `i64` directly.)  
     - `self.state.file_mtimes.insert(repo_rel.clone(), mtime_secs);` and `stats.files_indexed += 1;`.  
  5. If `row_buffer` is non-empty:  
     - Build the `RecordBatch`: instantiate one Arrow array per schema column (`StringArray::from_iter_values`, `Int32Array::from_iter`, `FixedSizeListArray::from_iter_primitive::<Float32Type, _, _>(...)` or equivalent flat-buffer construction, `TimestampMicrosecondArray::from_iter_values`), then `RecordBatch::try_new(branch_manager::code_chunks_schema(), columns)?`. Operator-readable error messages per WO-0051 lessons line 405 standing pattern.  
     - Wrap in `RecordBatchIterator::new(std::iter::once(Ok(record_batch)), branch_manager::code_chunks_schema())`.  
     - `table.add(Box::new(iter)).execute().await?` — appends rows to the LanceDB table.  
     - `stats.chunks_inserted = row_buffer.len();`.  
  6. `self.state.save_atomic(&self.state_path()).await?` (atomic JSON write).  
  7. `Ok(stats)`.  
  Define `struct RowDraft { ... }` privately inside `lancedb_indexer.rs` to keep the per-file iteration ergonomic.

Inside `lancedb_indexer.rs`, define `pub struct IndexerHandle` that owns a spawned tokio task driven by a `tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<crate::watcher::FileEvent>`. Constructor: `pub fn spawn(indexer: Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<LancedbChunkIndexer<S>>>, repo_root: PathBuf, mut events: tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<crate::watcher::FileEvent>) -> Self`. The spawned task loops on `while let Some(event) = events.recv().await { match event.kind { FileEventKind::Created | FileEventKind::Modified => { let _ = indexer.lock().await.index_paths(&repo_root, &[event.path]).await; }, _ => { /* ignore Removed/Renamed/Other for F04 — Removed/Renamed handled in a future WO */ } } }`. Returns `Self { join_handle: tokio::task::JoinHandle<()> }`. Add `pub fn shutdown(self) -> tokio::task::JoinHandle<()>` that returns the join handle so callers can `.await` graceful drain. The handle's job is to PROVE the 'background' / 'file changes trigger' half of F04's description without coupling the frozen unit test to the watcher's full backend wiring (kept orthogonal per WO-0026 lessons).

Add `pub mod lancedb_indexer;` to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` (at the appropriate alphabetical position — between `lifecycle` and `plugin_manager`). Add `pub use lancedb_indexer::{ChunkIndexerError, CodeRankEmbeddingSource, EmbeddingSource, EmbeddingSourceError, IndexerHandle, IndexerState, IndexerStats, LancedbChunkIndexer};` on a single line (use `#[rustfmt::skip]` if it exceeds rustfmt's 100-col wrap budget per WO-0056 lessons line 490 / WO-0058 line 549). Append a NEW trailing paragraph to the lib.rs preamble: '`lancedb_indexer` (introduced in WO-0064 for `P2-W8-F04`) owns the per-branch background chunk-indexing pipeline that consumes [`ucil_embeddings::EmbeddingChunker`] + [`ucil_embeddings::CodeRankEmbed`] (the latter behind the UCIL-internal [`lancedb_indexer::EmbeddingSource`] trait per DEC-0008 §4) and writes 12-column [`branch_manager::code_chunks_schema`]-conforming [`arrow_array::RecordBatch`] rows into the per-branch `code_chunks` LanceDB table opened by [`BranchManager::create_branch_table`] (master-plan §11.2 + §12.2). Incremental skip is driven by a `<branches_root>/<sanitised_branch>/indexer-state.json` mtime sidecar persisted via [`lancedb_indexer::IndexerState::save_atomic`]. The companion [`lancedb_indexer::IndexerHandle`] subscribes to a `tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<watcher::FileEvent>` and dispatches each create/modify event to the indexer — consumer wiring of the handle into [`watcher::FileWatcher`] is deferred to a follow-up WO. Frozen acceptance test [`executor::test_lancedb_incremental_indexing`] (DEC-0007 module-root) exercises 6 sub-assertions via a deterministic `TestEmbeddingSource` impl per DEC-0008.'

Promote `sanitise_branch_name` in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs:350` from `fn` to `pub fn` (one-line visibility change), per the WO scope_in step 8 'F04 IS the third caller' carve-out. The promotion is documented inline at branch_manager.rs:347-349 ('intentional duplication … is deferred to a follow-up ADR if a third caller materialises'). Re-export from `lib.rs` alongside the existing `branch_manager` re-exports: extend the existing `pub use branch_manager::{BranchManager, BranchManagerError, BranchTableInfo, code_chunks_schema, ARCHIVE_DIR_NAME};` line to add `sanitise_branch_name`. Document the promotion in the commit body citing the third-caller trigger from branch_manager.rs:347-349.

Add the frozen acceptance test `pub async fn test_lancedb_incremental_indexing()` annotated `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)]` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. The selector `executor::test_lancedb_incremental_indexing` resolves to `ucil_daemon::executor::test_lancedb_incremental_indexing` ONLY when the fn is column-0 in `executor.rs`. Verifier checks via `grep -nE '^(pub )?async fn test_lancedb_incremental_indexing\(' crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` returning a non-`mod tests {}`-nested line. Test setup:  
  (a) Define a private `struct TestEmbeddingSource { dim: usize }` (UCIL-internal trait impl per DEC-0008 §4 — NOT a critical-dep mock per WO-0048 lessons line 363 / WO-0063 scope_in step 7(a) precedent) inside an adjacent `#[cfg(test)] mod lancedb_indexer_test_support { ... }` block OR as adjacent module-root `#[cfg(test)]` items. Implement `EmbeddingSource` for it: `name() -> "test"`, `dim() -> self.dim`, `async fn embed(&self, code: &str) -> Result<Vec<f32>, EmbeddingSourceError>` returns a deterministic `Vec<f32>` of length `self.dim` derived from a hash of `code` (e.g. `let h = sha2::Sha256::digest(code.as_bytes()); (0..self.dim).map(|i| (h[i % 32] as f32) / 255.0).collect()` — deterministic, non-trivial, and depends on input so different chunks produce different vectors).  
  (b) Use `tempfile::TempDir` for the repo + branches roots: `let repo = tempfile::TempDir::new().expect("tmp repo"); let branches_root = repo.path().join(".ucil/branches"); tokio::fs::create_dir_all(&branches_root).await.expect("mkdir branches");`.  
  (c) Create the branch via `let mgr = std::sync::Arc::new(BranchManager::new(&branches_root)); mgr.create_branch_table("main", None).await.expect("create main");`.  
  (d) Create a real `EmbeddingChunker` via `let chunker = std::sync::Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(ucil_embeddings::EmbeddingChunker::new_with_synthetic_tokenizer().expect("synthetic chunker")));` — use the WO-0060-established `synthetic tokenizer via Tokenizer::from_str(tokenizer_json)` pattern (per WO-0060 lessons line 637) so the test does NOT require the production HF model artefacts. If `EmbeddingChunker` does not expose a `new_with_synthetic_tokenizer` constructor, the executor MAY add a `#[cfg(test)] pub fn new_with_synthetic_tokenizer() -> Result<Self, EmbeddingChunkerError>` shim to `ucil-embeddings::chunker.rs` — but PREFER constructing via the existing `pub fn new(...)` constructor with a tokenizer built inline via `tokenizers::Tokenizer::from_str(SIMPLE_WORDLEVEL_TOKENIZER_JSON)` directly in the test setup. Disclose the choice in the RFR's upstream-API research checklist.  
  (e) Create source files: write `repo.path().join("src/foo.rs")` with `pub fn foo() { let x = 1; let y = 2; let z = x + y; }` (multi-statement; chunker emits ≥1 chunk), `repo.path().join("src/bar.rs")` with `pub fn bar() -> i32 { 42 }`. Both files go through `tokio::fs::create_dir_all(parent)` then `tokio::fs::write(path, content).await`.  
  (f) Build `let source = Arc::new(TestEmbeddingSource { dim: 768 });` and `let mut indexer = LancedbChunkIndexer::new(mgr.clone(), "main", chunker.clone(), source.clone());`.  
  (g) **Sub-assertion 1 — first-pass indexes all**: `let stats1 = indexer.index_paths(repo.path(), &[repo.path().join("src/foo.rs"), repo.path().join("src/bar.rs")]).await.expect("first pass");` MUST satisfy `stats1.files_scanned == 2 && stats1.files_skipped_unchanged == 0 && stats1.files_indexed == 2 && stats1.chunks_inserted >= 2 && stats1.chunks_failed == 0`. Operator-readable panic per WO-0051 lessons line 405: `assert_eq!(stats1.files_indexed, 2, "first pass: expected files_indexed=2; got stats={stats1:?}")`.  
  (h) **Sub-assertion 2 — second pass skips unchanged**: `let stats2 = indexer.index_paths(repo.path(), &[repo.path().join("src/foo.rs"), repo.path().join("src/bar.rs")]).await.expect("second pass");` MUST satisfy `stats2.files_scanned == 2 && stats2.files_skipped_unchanged == 2 && stats2.files_indexed == 0 && stats2.chunks_inserted == 0`. Open the LanceDB table and `count_rows(None).await` MUST return the same count as after pass 1 (no duplicate inserts).  
  (i) **Sub-assertion 3 — touch then reindex only touched**: bump foo.rs's mtime by writing a NEW byte at the end (`tokio::fs::write(foo_path, format!("{}\nfn extra() {{}}\n", original_content)).await`), then `let stats3 = indexer.index_paths(repo.path(), &[foo_path.clone(), bar_path.clone()]).await.expect("third pass");`. MUST satisfy `stats3.files_skipped_unchanged == 1 && stats3.files_indexed == 1 && stats3.chunks_inserted >= 1`. Operator-readable panic on failure.  
  (j) **Sub-assertion 4 — file_hash differs on changed file**: re-open the LanceDB table, query rows for `file_path == "src/foo.rs"`, collect distinct `file_hash` values — MUST contain >1 distinct hash (the pre-touch hash AND the post-touch hash both present because `add` appends, doesn't replace — partial-update semantics deferred to a follow-up `merge_insert`-based WO). For `file_path == "src/bar.rs"`, MUST contain exactly 1 distinct hash.  
  (k) **Sub-assertion 5 — persisted state JSON roundtrips**: `let state_path = indexer.state_path(); let raw = tokio::fs::read_to_string(&state_path).await.expect("state.json exists"); let parsed: IndexerState = serde_json::from_str(&raw).expect("state.json parses");` MUST satisfy `parsed.file_mtimes.contains_key(&PathBuf::from("src/foo.rs")) && parsed.file_mtimes.contains_key(&PathBuf::from("src/bar.rs")) && parsed.schema_version == IndexerState::schema_version_current()`.  
  (l) **Sub-assertion 6 — embedding column is FixedSizeList<Float32, 768>**: re-open the LanceDB table, `let schema = table.schema().await.expect("schema"); let embedding_field = schema.field_with_name("embedding").expect("embedding column"); match embedding_field.data_type() { arrow_schema::DataType::FixedSizeList(inner, 768) => { assert_eq!(inner.data_type(), &arrow_schema::DataType::Float32, "embedding inner type Float32; got {:?}", inner.data_type()); }, other => panic!("embedding column not FixedSizeList<Float32, 768>; got {:?}", other) }`. Proves the schema contract holds end-to-end.

Add a SECOND module-root test `pub async fn test_lancedb_indexer_handle_processes_events()` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs`. The test exercises the `IndexerHandle::spawn` watcher→indexer wiring:  
  (a) Setup as in `test_lancedb_incremental_indexing` (tempdir, BranchManager, chunker, TestEmbeddingSource).  
  (b) Create one source file `src/foo.rs`.  
  (c) Build `let indexer = Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(LancedbChunkIndexer::new(...)));`.  
  (d) Create channel `let (tx, rx) = tokio::sync::mpsc::channel::<crate::watcher::FileEvent>(8);`.  
  (e) Spawn handle: `let handle = IndexerHandle::spawn(indexer.clone(), repo.path().to_owned(), rx);`.  
  (f) Send a `FileEvent { kind: FileEventKind::Created, path: repo.path().join("src/foo.rs"), source: FileEventSource::Watcher }` over `tx`.  
  (g) `drop(tx);` to close the channel.  
  (h) `handle.shutdown().await.expect("join");` — graceful drain.  
  (i) Open the LanceDB table; assert `count_rows(None).await >= 1` (the foo.rs chunks landed via the handle's dispatch).  
  Operator-readable panic on each assertion. This proves the handle's loop actually invokes `index_paths` — not just compiles.

HOWEVER, the LanceDB count_rows query in SA2's 'no duplicate inserts' check would return 0 (NOT the post-pass-1 row count), failing that assertion. **No `cargo-mutants` per-WO gate.** DEC-0007 removed it; pre-baked function-body mutations in `acceptance` are the authoritative anti-laziness layer (carry from WO-0042 → WO-0063 — now 15 consecutive cleared with the new pre-baked-mutation discipline).
scope_out: **No watcher-backend wiring of the indexer-handle into `crate::watcher::FileWatcher`.** F04 ships the `IndexerHandle::spawn(...)` API + a unit test that drives it via a hand-fed channel. The actual wiring of `FileWatcher`'s output `Receiver<FileEvent>` into `IndexerHandle::spawn` lives in a follow-up WO (likely the daemon's `main.rs` startup flow, or a NEW `IndexerSupervisor` orchestrator). The test `executor::test_lancedb_indexer_handle_processes_events` proves the handle's loop works; production wiring is the next site.

**No ANN index creation on the LanceDB table.** F04 writes plain row appends; the IVF/HNSW index for fast vector search is created in P2-W8-F07 (vector query latency benchmark) and consumed in P2-W8-F08 (`find_similar` MCP tool). LanceDB's brute-force scan over a small fixture is sufficient for SA2's count check.

**No row deletion on file Removed/Renamed events.** F04's `IndexerHandle` ignores `FileEventKind::Removed` and `FileEventKind::Renamed`. Stale rows for deleted files persist until a future WO adds a `delete_by_file_path` pass. Row count is monotonically non-decreasing in F04. Documented inline in the handle's loop body.

**No `merge_insert` semantics.** F04 uses `Table::add` (append-only) so a re-indexed file's NEW rows coexist with the OLD rows for that file_path. SA4's 'distinct file_hash count > 1' assertion exercises this exactly. A future WO will switch to `merge_insert` keyed on `(file_path, id)` for true upsert semantics. Documented inline.
**No `find_similar` MCP tool wiring.** That's P2-W8-F08 (separate WO). F04 ONLY ships the indexer + handle; the MCP-side query path is orthogonal.
**No vector query latency benchmark.** That's P2-W8-F07 (separate WO).

**No real CodeRankEmbed model artefact requirement in the frozen test.** SA1-SA6 use `TestEmbeddingSource` (deterministic synthetic vectors). The production `CodeRankEmbeddingSource` IS implemented and unit-tested in `lancedb_indexer.rs::tests` for compile-correctness and `EmbeddingSource` trait conformance, but invoking the actual model requires `ml/models/coderankembed/{model.onnx,tokenizer.json}` (gitignored, downloaded by `scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh`). A SEPARATE optional test gated on artefact presence MAY be added but is NOT required for AC.
**No daemon `main.rs` plumbing.** The `IndexerSupervisor` boot sequence (subscribe to watcher, spawn one IndexerHandle per branch, etc.) is a follow-up WO. F04 keeps the new module self-contained.

**No changes to `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**`, `crates/ucil-core/**`, `crates/ucil-cli/**`, `crates/ucil-agents/**`, `adapters/**`, `ml/**`, `plugins/**`, `tests/fixtures/**`, `tests/integration/**`, `tests/scenarios/**`, `tests/benchmarks/**`** — F04 is contained to: `Cargo.toml` (workspace-level sha2 add), `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` (ucil-embeddings + sha2 deps), `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lancedb_indexer.rs` (NEW), `crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs` (one-line `pub fn` promotion), `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` (two NEW module-root tests), `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` (mod + re-exports), `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F04.sh` (NEW), `ucil-build/work-orders/0064-ready-for-review.md` (NEW). Optionally `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/chunker.rs` if a `#[cfg(test)] new_with_synthetic_tokenizer` shim is added per scope_in step 12(d) — preferred path is to construct the tokenizer inline in the daemon test setup so this file is NOT touched.

**No new ADR.** DEC-0005 (module-coherence commits up to ~500 LOC), DEC-0007 (frozen-selector module-root placement), DEC-0008 (UCIL-internal trait boundaries — `EmbeddingSource` is the seam), DEC-0011 (crate-scoped test-path guard), DEC-0016 §Closed (orphan-branch resolved at `57e50ab`) all cover the WO. If a NEW pattern surfaces (e.g. lancedb 0.16's `Table::add` API differs from the assumed shape, or the `ucil-embeddings` dep introduces an `ort` build-flag conflict that breaks `crates/ucil-daemon` compilation, or `EmbeddingChunker::new` cannot construct a synthetic-tokenizer impl without an `ahash`-style transitive dep), STOP and write a follow-up ADR before working around. Cite scope_out[6] from WO-0058 'if a NEW pattern surfaces … STOP and write an ADR before working around' as the standing escalation trigger.

**No P2-W8-F07 / F08 work in this WO.** F07 (vector query latency benchmark) and F08 (`find_similar` MCP tool) both depend on F04 and will be planned in subsequent WOs after F04 is verified-green and merged.
